description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: Code_analysis tags partially synced: 67 WINs have curmudgeon reviews but 3 still lack reviewed=true tags (WIN-032, WIN-033, WIN-065). Run: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace to sync.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: INFRASTRUCTURE: 59/67 WINs synced; sync gap due to partial code_analysis review cycle. Requires running curmudgeon sync script (pending infrastructure build). Mark as deferred infrastructure task.
issues.fix_details: code_analysis_tags added to WIN-032, WIN-033, WIN-065 curmudgeon review files (2026-04-06 decider run). Apply via: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace. Correct tags: WIN-032 (none/false/true/false), WIN-033 (none/true/true/false), WIN-065 (none/true/true/false). Close after running sync script.

issues.description: Two external links broken: LPI Apollo 11 LRR (404), CUORE experiment site (expired SSL). Not yet fixed by ISS-032.
issues.fix_details: LPI link (WIN-020) resolved per integrity report 2026-04-06 ("lpi.usra.edu: all resolve OK now"). CUORE link (WIN-015) patched in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T06-35.json (removes incorrect CUORE sentence). Both components now addressed.

issues.description: SEC-3.1 Live Power page not archived. Our 20-domain classification is unverifiable against the dome's source page. Need to extract and archive the page to raw-text/.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: DEFERRED: Requires poller to extract the Live Power page content. Flag for next poller enhancement.

issues.description: SEC-3.1 never explains what λ_g was fitted to (H(r) curve with 2 free parameters). Also statistical independence argument oversimplified — real issue is no out-of-sample prediction, not parameter sharing per se.

SEC-3.1 Domain #2 (Tesla) should cross-reference WIN-001 finding that patent 787412 doesn't contain the cited formula. Also section doesn't reference code_analysis tag data (4/58 derive from dome, 35/58 relabel standard).

SEC-3.2: Eclipse prediction range stale. Section claims dome predicts "-17 to -21 nT" but dome's registered predictions use dual-baseline system with 9 per-station values: BOU baseline (-5 to -13 nT) and W004 baseline (-10 to -26 nT). We're attacking a simplified version.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: SEC-3.2 PROSE REWRITE NEEDED: Eclipse prediction range stale. Dome uses dual-baseline system (+17 nT baseline correction). Section text needs rewrite to reflect registered forecast format.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T15-53.json

issues.description: SEC-3.2: E-PRED-B (dome's strongest counterargument) completely ignored. Predicts Hartland > Ebro despite lower eclipse coverage — a relative prediction derived from dome geometry independent of absolute baselines.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: SEC-3.2 CRITICAL GAP: E-PRED-B (dome's strongest counterargument) completely absent from our analysis. Dome predicts Hartland > Ebro despite lower eclipse coverage. Requires section rewrite to engage this prediction.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T15-53.json

issues.description: SEC-3.2: Claims dome "applied his correction factor of 1.672" but 1.672 is KAPPA coupling constant (WIN-012), not the eclipse FSF. Actual eclipse uses station-specific FSF values (0.642-2.075) derived from H(r)/r.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: SEC-3.2 FACTUAL ERROR: Text claims dome "applied his correction factor of 1.672" but 1.672 is KAPPA (WIN-012), not eclipse factor. Requires correction to actual eclipse FSF value (~0.84).
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T15-53.json

issues.description: SEC-3.2: Kill-Shot Test 5 cross-references Section 3.2 for eclipse PATH prediction, but Section 3.2 discusses MAGNETIC ANOMALY prediction — cross-reference mismatch.
SEC-3.2: "0.0 nT exactly" globe prediction quote may not exist verbatim on dome site. Need verification before publication.
SEC-3.5.4: WIN-024 tagging inconsistency — section treats it as relabeling but wins.json has relabels_standard=false.
SEC-3.5.4: Missing V51.0 timing context — five tidal WINs added 2026-04-05, hours before review launched. Strategic padding not flagged.
SEC-3.5.4: WIN-064 code_analysis=null (not yet reviewed). Add footnote noting pending status.
SEC-3.5.4: Missing aggregate statistic — 35/58 reviewed WINs (60%) relabel standard physics. Systemic proportion not highlighted.

Framing inconsistency between HTML and DOCX: The DOCX (build-doc-v4.js line 495) frames WIN-056 as: 'H(r) was fitted to match solar observations, then solar elevation is re-derived from H(r). This is circular.' The HTML detail focuses on globe formula substitution (23.45° axial tilt). These are rela

WIN-057 and WIN-058 are conflated in the build doc. The build-doc-v4.js file states: 'WIN-057/058 (two-zone disc / angular coordinate): V13 two-zone topology improved cross-equatorial routes but regressed northern hemisphere accuracy from 5.2% to 7.3%. An internal coordinate convention is not a phys

SEC-4.5.3 HTML says dome resolves gravity contradiction with globe formula g = GM / r². Dome actually uses WGS84 empirical surface values (g = 9.7803 − 0.0325·cos(2φ)). Section 4.5.8 correctly identifies this formula. Straw-man error. Curmudgeon review: SEC-4.5.3, 2026-04-06T22:45:00Z.
issues.fix_details: Patch produced in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T15-30.json

issues.description: Section numbering mismatch between HTML and DOCX in Part 4.5: HTML 4.5.3 = Gravity at Rim, HTML 4.5.4 = Solar Diameter. DOCX 4.5.3 = Solar Angular Diameter, DOCX 4.5.4 = Tidal Pattern. Kill-shot table cross-reference row 11 references Section 4.5.3 — broken in one format. Curmudgeon review: SEC-4.5.3/4.5.4.

SEC-4.5.9 CRITICAL FACTUAL ERROR: Section claims "d_geo is undefined" as the first technical argument. This is FALSE for V13. The dome's V13 coordinates page explicitly defines d_geo: EW_arc = 4×a×E(e²)×(Δlon/360)×(r_avg/a), NS = |r₁−r₂|, d_geo = √(NS²+EW_arc²), where a=20,015 km, e=0.66. A dome defender can immediately show we didn't read their page. This is the lead argument in the section — failing it damages the entire section's credibility.
issues.fix_details: Rewrite: d_geo IS defined but (1) parameters a=20,015 km and e=0.66 are fitted to WGS84 data, (2) inputs (lat/lon) are WGS84-derived, (3) n(r) remains undefined. The real critique is circular derivation, not undefined formula. Prose section issue — cannot auto-patch; requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.5.9 MAJOR CATEGORY ERROR: Comparing scaffold Euclidean distance (410 km) between Christchurch and Greymouth to TranzAlpine rail distance (223 km) is comparing straight-line distance to a winding mountain route. Correct comparison is to great-circle distance (~160 km), which shows 156% overshoot — even worse for our argument.
issues.fix_details: Replace rail distance (223 km) with great-circle distance (~160 km) as the correct comparison. 410/160 = 256% of great-circle distance. This actually makes the argument stronger. Prose section — requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.5.9 MAJOR OVERSTATEMENT: Claiming a genuine model would have "one version" is easily refuted (Newton, Einstein, Bohr all iterated their models). Dome defender can use this to make us look unreasonable.
issues.fix_details: Replace "one version" argument with the correct critique: overfitting known data vs. predicting new data. Scientific models iterate, but they iterate because predictions fail — the dome's V13 coordinate system was not motivated by a failed prediction. Prose section — requires editing generate-html.js Section 4.5.9.

issues.description: SEC-4.8 MAJOR SELF-CONTRADICTION: Section claims sun appears "50% larger at noon" AND "30% distance variation = 30% size variation" — these are mathematically inconsistent. If the sun is 30% closer at noon than at horizon, angular size increases by 1/0.70 = 43%, not 50%. The actual dome geometry gives a 48% reduction at 90° hour angle (sun at horizon vs. overhead in flat-earth geometry). Our text contains a numerical inconsistency in a section critiquing numerical inconsistencies.
issues.fix_details: Queued as EXP-004 for analyst: full rewrite of Section 4.8 solar angular diameter kill-shot

issues.description: SEC-4.8 MAJOR MISSING DEFENSE: Section never addresses the dome's pre-built aetheric refraction defense (AI Context Rule 11). A kill-shot that ignores the target's known escape route is ineffective. Dome can claim aetheric refraction corrects the observed size variation.
issues.fix_details: Queued as EXP-004 for analyst: full rewrite of Section 4.8 solar angular diameter kill-shot

issues.description: DOCX MISSING SECTIONS 5.14-5.16: The Word/PDF document (build-doc-v4.js) completely omits Sections 5.14 (Repository Infrastructure: Steering AI), 5.15 (The Repository Is the Model), and 5.16 (The Monitoring Infrastructure). These three sections contain the review's most original contribution — the inject_ai_layer.py analysis, curve-fitting exposure, and blockchain timestamping critique. Any reader using the Word/PDF document misses the entire repository code analysis argument. This has been flagged by the curmudgeon in 4 consecutive section reviews (SEC-5.14, 5.15, 5.16, and repeated in 5.16 with priority escalation to critical). Requires adding Sections 5.14-5.16 to build-doc-v4.js.
issues.fix_details: Add Sections 5.14, 5.15, and 5.16 to build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js. These are prose sections — cannot be auto-patched. Requires manual editing of the document builder.

issues.description: DOME SOURCE FILES NOT ARCHIVED: Sections 5.14 and 5.16 make specific claims about Python scripts (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py, monitor.py, recalc_v51.py, app.py, test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py) including exact quotes, line references, and formula citations. None of these files are preserved in raw-text/. If the dome author modifies or removes these scripts, our claims become unsubstantiated assertions — especially ironic given Section 5.16 criticizes the dome for not archiving its predictions. The -29.1 nT calculation in Section 5.16 cites monitor.py which is unarchived, making the specific formula unverifiable. Curmudgeon flagged this as the highest-priority unresolved citation issue.
issues.fix_details: Archive all referenced Python source files from the dome's GitHub repository in raw-text/. Suggested: raw-text/dome-repo-scripts/monitor.py, inject_ai_layer.py, etc. After archiving, verify the -29.1 nT calculation FSF and the specific formula attributions. URL: https://github.com/john09289/predictions

issues.description: CUORE EXPERIMENT LINK BROKEN: https://cuore.lngs.infn.it/ returns HTTP 404. CUORE is cited in the context of WIN-015 (and possibly elsewhere) as an underground laboratory detector. The CUORE experiment may have moved its website or the LNGS portal was reorganized. Integrity check confirmed this on 2026-04-06.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T06-35.json: removes CUORE sentence from WIN-015 detail_evidence. CUORE is a neutrinoless double beta decay experiment unrelated to Faraday attenuation.

issues.description: GFZ CHAMP MISSION LINK BROKEN: https://op.gfz-potsdam.de/champ/ returns HTTP 404. The CHAMP mission archive appears to have been reorganized at GFZ Potsdam. CHAMP is cited as a geomagnetic/gravity satellite mission. Integrity check confirmed this on 2026-04-06.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T06-35.json: updates WIN-039 CHAMP URL from op.gfz-potsdam.de/champ/ to new GFZ URL. Consistent with WIN-029 which already uses the new URL.

issues.description: SEC-4.9 REFRACTION CLAIM OVERSTATED: Section states "At least six WINs rely on aetheric refraction to avoid falsification" citing WIN-016, 017, 026, 033, 056, 065. Verification of current WIN detail text shows only WIN-016 and WIN-065 explicitly mention aetheric refraction or n(r). WIN-017 (stellar parallax) discusses firmament wobble; WIN-026 (crepuscular rays) discusses perspective effects; WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis) discusses intrinsic luminosity; WIN-056 (solar elevation) discusses globe formula substitution. A dome defender checking the citation would find only 2 of 6 WINs explicitly invoke refraction in our own text. The logical argument (dome geometry WOULD require refraction) is valid but our text does not make that dependency explicit for WIN-017/026/033/056. Section 4.9 requires editing generate-html.js: either (a) add explicit refraction dependency notes to WIN-017/026/033/056 detail text, or (b) soften Section 4.9 to "would require aetheric refraction or equivalent unfalsifiable mechanism."
issues.fix_details: Two options: (a) Preferred — add brief notes to WIN-017/026/033/056 detail text explaining WHY dome geometry requires aetheric refraction for each, then Section 4.9 claim is fully supported. (b) Fallback — soften Section 4.9 count from six to two explicitly, add "at least four more would require equivalent unfalsifiable escape hatches." Option (a) strengthens both the individual WIN arguments and Section 4.9.

issues.description: DOCX Self-Contradicted count hardcoded as 11 in build-doc-v4.js (Pattern 11 text line ~791, summary paragraph line ~812, version history line ~859), but wins.json now has 12 Self-Contradicted WINs. The HTML correctly uses computed counts. Violates project rule on hardcoding counts.

SEC-6.2 conclusions (generate-html.js line ~1473) repeats eclipse prediction factual errors: states dome range was derived with 1.672x scaling factor (1.672 is KAPPA, not eclipse scaling factor), describes single -17 to -21 nT prediction instead of dual-baseline -5 to -26 nT system, and references stale GUA 2016 baseline. Same errors as ISS-082/083/084 in SEC-3.2 but in the conclusions section which is most-read. ISS-082/083/084 patches were written but not yet applied to HTML.

SEC-5.16 (line ~1446) and SEC-6.2: Unverifiable claim that monitor.py computes delta_B = -18.22 x 0.95 x 1.672 = -29.1 nT. monitor.py not archived, FSF 1.672 does not match Ebro listed FSF (1.029), specific code line cannot be verified. Replace with verifiable HAD station discrepancy (-26.2 nT vs advertised -17 to -21 nT) or archive monitor.py first.

SEC-6.2 conclusions never address dome E-PRED tests A-D. E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro despite lower spatial coverage) is dome-specific FSF geometry and is the dome's strongest discriminating counterargument. Conclusion vulnerable to: "They never addressed our actual discriminating tests." Fix: Add response explaining Chapman conductivity effects depend on local Sq geometry, not just coverage fraction.

Integrity report (2026-04-06T14:46Z): 2 broken external links: (1) https://cuore.lngs.infn.it/ returns HTTP 404 - CUORE experiment site may have moved. (2) https://op.gfz-potsdam.de/champ/ returns HTTP 404 - GFZ CHAMP mission archive reorganized. Need updated URLs for both.
issues.fix_details: CUORE fix (WIN-015) and CHAMP fix (WIN-039) both patched in suggested-patches-2026-04-06T06-35.json. Integrity report links should resolve after applying patches and rebuilding.

issues.description: SEC-6.2 DOCX: Curmudgeon reports cross-reference error - DOCX version references "Part 3.1" instead of "Section 3.2" for eclipse analysis. HTML version correct. Fix: Check build-doc-v4.js eclipse conclusion paragraph for Part 3.1 and update to Section 3.2.

KILLSHOT-GAIA missing aetheric refraction defense: Section does not mention aetheric refraction, even though Section 4.9 explicitly lists WIN-017 (parallax) as depending on it. Should cross-reference Section 4.9 and explain why position-dependent refraction cannot rescue the parallax argument (refraction depends on observer position, not star distance).
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing New Horizons parallax experiment (April 2020): NASA measured stellar parallax from 7-billion-km baseline (Proxima Cen: 32.4 arcsec, Wolf 359: 15.7 arcsec). Physically impossible if stars are on a dome 9,086 km high. Baseline (7B km) is ~800,000 times the firmament height; measurement made from beyond any hypothetical dome, eliminating aetheric refraction as escape hatch.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing differential-parallax argument: In any small patch of sky, Gaia measures stars with parallaxes differing by 100-1000x. Any dome motion (rigid wobble, flexible undulation, or refraction) would affect all objects in the same direction similarly. Per-star parallax variation perfectly correlated with independently determined distance is impossible under any dome mechanism.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-006 (KILLSHOT-GAIA section strengthening). See monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

issues.description: KILLSHOT-GAIA missing scale comparison: Proxima Centauri parallax-derived distance (~4.02e13 km) is approximately 4.7 billion times the dome maximum firmament height (8,537 km). This ratio should be stated explicitly.
KILLSHOT-GAIA: Section refers to "The Gaia space telescope" — Gaia is technically a space observatory/astrometric satellite (ESA nomenclature). Minor imprecision.

KILLSHOT-GAIA missing from DOCX/PDF: Section 4.3 (Gaia kill-shot) appears in HTML but build-doc-v4.js has no corresponding section. The Word/PDF download is missing one of the six kill-shots entirely. Needs a new section added to build-doc-v4.js after the GPS kill-shot.

WIN-054 (El Gordo) verdict should change from 'Misleading' to 'Not Demonstrated'. El Gordo is a genuine 6.2σ ΛCDM anomaly — the dome isn't misrepresenting data, it's failing to offer alternative physics that explains the discrepancy. Dome's argument is 'this anomaly exists therefore dome is possible,' not 'dome predicts this anomaly.' Lacks derivation from dome geometry or testable alternative mechanism.
issues.fix_details: Verdict changed Misleading->Not Demonstrated. Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json. Note: ISS-441 removed from EXP-005 scope; EXP-005 continues for ISS-442/443/444.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.

INCONSISTENT VERDICT BOUNDARY: WIN-043 and WIN-060 are classified 'Std Model Explains' but their findings explicitly mention 'duplicate of WIN-007' and 'duplicate of WIN-004/005'. Duplication is a 'Misleading' criterion per this review. Either remove duplication language from findings or reclassify these WINs to 'Misleading'. Current state violates verdict taxonomy consistency.

WIN-058 verdict mismatch: Currently classified 'Misleading' but detail_verdict_text describes it as 'Internal coordinate convention, not a physical prediction' which logically fits 'Not Demonstrated' better. If the argument is that dome's two-zone coordinate system is a mathematical curve-fit with no physical justification, that's an unfalsifiable convention (Not Demonstrated), not a misrepresentation (Misleading). Either change verdict to 'Not Demonstrated' or revise finding language to emphasize curve-fitting deception rather than mere convention.

TIDAL SELF-CONTRADICTION GROUPING: 5 tidal WINs (045–051) share one underlying geometric argument (local moon at 2,534 km cannot produce semidiurnal two-bulge pattern regardless of mechanism). Yet counted as 5 separate self-contradictions in verdict tally. This is exactly the methodological flaw we criticize the dome for (tallying duplicated WINs). Section 4.5.4 should include acknowledgment note: 'These five WINs derive from a single geometric constraint' to maintain analytical integrity.

WIN-023 (Schumann resonance ~950 CE): Detail_finding does not acknowledge that the geophysical timing claim (magnetic pole reversal ~950 AD) IS supported by archaeomagnetic data. Current text focuses on frequency mismatch (7.83 Hz observed vs 22 Hz dome prediction). Missing the kernel of truth: dome correctly identified real paleomagnetic event, then wrongly attributes timing to their Schumann resonance mechanism. Vulnerable to objection: 'You're ignoring our correct prediction about the paleomagnetic transition timing.' Add nuanced finding.
issues.fix_details: Finding and detail_verdict_text updated to acknowledge kernel of truth (real paleomagnetic event) before critique. Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json.

issues.description: VERDICT LEGEND ORDERING ARBITRARY: Current verdict order in legend appears unsystematic. Recommend ordering by evidential force: (1) Wrong on Own Terms (Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data) → (2) Not Evidence (Std Model Explains, Misleading) → (3) Not Evaluable (Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable). This grouping reflects logical hierarchy: Can the dome contradict itself? → Can the dome offer new evidence? → Can the dome be tested? Current legend order does not reflect this structure.

NOT DEMONSTRATED DEFINITION INCOMPLETE: Current definition covers 'unreplicated fringe experiments' but should expand to include 'claim made without derivation from model geometry' or 'adopted from external source without independent justification.' Many dome WINs adopt published observations then claim them as predictions without showing how dome geometry predicts the specific observation. Example: WIN-054 (El Gordo) notes the anomaly exists, but dome never derives why dome geometry specifically predicts an El Gordo-scale anomaly rather than some other value. Broader definition would make this WIN a natural 'Not Demonstrated' fit.

WIN-045 cross-references 'Section 4.5.4' (Solar Diameter) instead of 'Section 4.5.2' (Tidal Pattern). Readers following the link land in the wrong section. WIN-046, WIN-050, WIN-051 already correctly reference Section 4.5.2; WIN-045 and WIN-049 are outliers from the holistic cross-reference audit.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json: change display text from Section 4.5.4 to Section 4.5.2.

issues.description: WIN-049 cross-references 'Section 4.5.4' (Solar Diameter) instead of 'Section 4.5.2' (Tidal Pattern). Same error as WIN-045. All five tidal WINs (045-051) should reference the Tidal Pattern section where the geometric semidiurnal argument is developed.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json: change display text from Section 4.5.4 to Section 4.5.2.

issues.description: HOL-TONE: WIN-001 detail_evidence uses 'smuggles in a globe parameter under a new name' — loaded language imputing deliberate deception. Evaluation Guide Principle 2 commits the review to distinguishing flawed from fraudulent. Replace with 'incorporates a globe parameter under a new name.' Substantive critique unchanged.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json: 'smuggles in' -> 'incorporates'.

issues.description: HOL-TONE: WIN-002 detail_evidence uses 'pretending the dome has uniform height' — loaded language imputing intent. Replace with 'treating the dome as having uniform height.' The surrounding critique (V12 key discovery contradicts this assumption) fully preserves the substantive point without asserting deception.
issues.fix_details: Patch in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T17-30.json: 'pretending' -> 'treating ... as having'.

issues.description: HOL-HIERARCHY (curmudgeon alert 2026-04-07T15:30): Argument hierarchy is inverted for persuasive force. Self-contradiction arguments (Schumann WIN-002, Tidal WIN-045/046/049/050/051, Gravity at rim WIN-067) are in Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions tab, 8th of 10 tabs). External falsification arguments (weaker — vulnerable to frame-of-reference objections) are in Part 4 (tabs 2-3). Readers encounter the weaker external arguments first and may never reach the dome's own geometry refuting itself. Restructuring Part 4 to lead with self-contradictions would significantly improve persuasive impact without changing any content.
issues.fix_details: Assigned to analyst as EXP-007. Requires structural HTML rewrite of tab ordering and section sequencing in generate-html.js.
